item.title: My first top vs my best top. Yes, I’m back! I have big big big things planned. More in photo description.
item.description:
To those that know me and appreciate and support me, I am asking for a few donations to help get my project up and running. So my buy me a coffee is buymeacoffee.com/itsyarnfool but I’m going to be fixing that title soon lol. Anything is appreciated! To those who crochet, I’m finally doing it, the website is a day from launch. To those who crochet. It’s going to have tips and tricks on literally everything you could think of. The point and hope of it all is going to be things you wish you knew before. Things I learned the hard way.
The second point to my website is modern stitches. I’ll say it for the bitches in the back WE ARE TIRED OF THE SAME STITCHES!! So I’ve dug and dug and dug for months to find new modern stitches and I have about 50 so far. I will release about 3 a week. So if you’re interested in that let me know in the comments and I’ll come back and absolutely link you to the site! I have a patreon called talk crochet to me for those that want exclusive access to my patterns or anything I end up putting on Etsy. My site will be add free, so the patreon also helps to keep a bit of monetary flow for me as this is now my full time job. I’m. NOT stopping so low as to fill my site with spam ads. That’s not me. Anyways, it’s gonna be super dope. And it’s unplugged so don’t expect me to act any other way than I do here.
item.dc:creator: TalkCrochetToMe

item.title: I love my in-laws.
item.description: https://imgur.com/gallery/Bt3SDYN Edit: I felt embarrassed because when I opened up to my own mother about it, she scoffed at me, rolled her eyes, said there's nothing wrong with me and that my psychiatrist is just treating me like a guinea pig. She said my mind isn't strong enough because I don't eat healthy enough. I eat super healthy. I felt like shit. Made me not want to let my husband's family know.

item.dc:creator: AjKaramba

item.title: 10,000 days clean/sober today
item.description:
Sorry for the selfie. I don’t have pics of me at my worst. Too scary. But randomly, my sister sent this after digitizing some oldies. This was New Year’s Eve on a cruise ship I think. I was high on everything I could find. In this photo alone I had lsd, cocaine, some champagne, weed, and who knows what else. A month later I’d be arrested by the DEAf or selling LSD in a sting operation. Someone I sold to, sold that to someone else and that person got busted then turned for a lighter sentence. My phone was tapped and they followed me. The bust was similar to the end of Goodfellas but without the helicopter. I was facing 80 years in jail. Luckily it was my first offense and I started treatment right away. The judge gave me a break but I would face the same charges if I got in trouble again. Huge fine, drivers license stripped, probation, rehab, etc. I never looked back and have been clean since. No more meth, crack, pcp, k, or Dramamine (when I couldn’t find anything else). Many of my “friends” disappeared but the true connections endured and thrived. My connection with family is amazing now. I have a life I love that could never have imagined then. I’ve had many adventures and multiple careers since. If you’re struggling. Know you can do it. I believe in you.
Dog tax. Ruby and her boyfriend. She’s so happy with him and he just wants to nap.
item.dc:creator: bendistraw

item.title: I was in St. Louis and ended up taking probably my favorite picture of The Arch.
item.description: Edit: Holy cow! Front page? I'm going to have to have this picture framed. Thanks for the kind words! Some context - The blue light on the trees are from some police cars parked off to the side (there was a mild fender bender or something and they had that lane blocked off). I was walking back to my hotel after pigging out and thought it was a neat view.
item.dc:creator: Eldibs

item.title: Fuck you Netflix!
item.description: Rant time! Okay, I understand that tech companies cannot keep their services compatible with all (older) hardware devices out there for ever. But the arrogant tone and blatant lie in this email rubbed me the wrong way. Netflix: you cannot use your device anymore to watch our shows and films. But please understand that we do this to so you can maintain the best possible viewing experience. Huh? What?? No!! The best possible viewing experience would be if I can keep using the tv I have always used to watch Netflix on. It always worked, why would you change it so that it doesn’t work anymore, and then claim that it will improve my viewing experience?? Get the fuck out of here with that bullshit. Time to find my old buccaneer hat, and set sail for the Bay! Ok. Rant over. Thanks for listening Imgur. I needed to get that out of my system.
item.dc:creator: BigBlueWonder

item.title: Project 2025
item.description: https://x.com/thetrueshelby/status/1809982480721518606 Project 2025 is the heritage foundation’s blueprint for a second trump term. Among its 900 odd pages of evil are plans to subjugate woman with federal bans on abortion and contraception, and even talk of taking away their right to vote. It’s actual handmaid’s tale style shit. Trump’s Supreme Court has already begun the process by giving him immunity for his future acts in a second term. He denies having anything to do with project 2025 or wanting to implement it, both of which are absolutely lies, as is always the case with him. Drop all the bullshit about Biden being old, or sounding rough. It’s him or Christian nationalist fascism. Vote blue now or you’re never voting again.
item.dc:creator: Thelrishlnquistion

item.title: Tories are mad that the new deputy Prime minister is *checks notes* happy and dancing in "promiscuous" shorts... JFC
item.dc:creator: softspokensatan

item.title: I'm cancer free!
item.description: Back in April, I was diagnosed with colon cancer. A mass was detected in my lower sigmoid colon, and after going through numerous tests and scans, it was determined that I needed surgery. Last Friday, doctors removed about a foot of my colon, and what pathology revealed to have been a stage 2 tumor. No other organs were affected. And as per my post-op meeting this morning, I will not be needing chemotherapy or radiation treatments, just occasional checkups. I was lucky. My advice to you: GET TESTED. I was experiencing zero symptoms. There was no pain, no discomfort, no blood, and no family history. I had no idea anything was abnormal. I only went because I’m over 50, and that’s what you’re supposed to do. Had I waited much longer, I would have had a very different and more grim prognosis. In fact, had I been tested at 40, or 45, as they now recommend, it would have been limited to an outpatient procedure.
item.dc:creator: Mindcrime

item.dc:creator: Skullwriter

item.title: Arizona for Abortion Access turned in more than twice the number of signatures needed to put their constitutional amendment on the ballot. Republicans should be worried. 1 in 5 voters signed to have a say on abortion.
item.description: Arizonans working to ensure the right to an abortion on Wednesday put an exclamation point on their march to the November ballot. 823,685 points, actually. That’s the number of signatures the Arizona for Abortion Access campaign turned in on petitions to overrule the Republican legislators who tried — and, fortunately, failed — to take us back to 1864. The ones who think that anyone who helps a woman obtain an abortion should spend a mandatory two to five years in prison. Never mind if the woman is a girl. Never mind if she’s a victim of rape. The message on Wednesday to those Republicans seems obvious. 1 in 5 voters signed to have a say on abortion. Arizona Republicans sorely miscalculated Polls show that most Arizonans support abortion, to a point. Clearly, that point is not in the 19th century, despite the hopes and dreams of the Republicans who have long controlled the Legislature. They sorely miscalculated when they pushed to keep that 1864 law, rather than repealing it and returning to the state’s 15-week ban, which also makes no exception for rape or incest. Then they failed, anyway, as five Republicans — four of them in competitive races — voted with Democrats to repeal that 1864 law and restore the 15-week ban they passed in 2022. Under the ballot proposal, abortion would become a constitutional right in Arizona, guaranteed until the point of viability and beyond in some cases. If I were Kari Lake, the U.S. Senate candidate who once called the 1864 ban a “great law,” I’d be frantic. If I were U.S. Rep. David Schweikert, the six-time co-sponsor of the “Life Begins at Conception Act” that would have outlawed abortion nationwide, I’d be trying to change the subject. Ditto for U.S. Rep. Juan Ciscomani, who like Schweikert faces a competitive challenge in November. The fallout will be fast, deep and likely inevitable Same for the Republican legislators who are clinging to a bare one-vote margin in both the state House and Senate. And for Supreme Court Justices Clint Bolick and Kathryn King, two of the four judges who reinstated the 19th century abortion ban — the two who must stand for retention on the November ballot. https://www.azcentral.com/story/opinion/op-ed/laurieroberts/2024/07/03/arizona-abortion-initiative-boost-signatures-ballot/74293965007/
item.dc:creator: Jbelkin

that's my time.. have a great night gang!!

item.title: 96 pounds down!
item.description: Done the old fashioned way, tracking calories, calorie deficit and weight lifting + light cardio are all it took! Plus the mindset and believing that you can do it!

item.title: Fresh daily dump for your coffee #249 (Meme Monday)
item.description:
1. Pretty much
2. That chemical reactions in your brain for half a second that thought it was gonna see boobs
3. More office daddy
4. Who dresses these people?
5
6. Bonus points if they wife is smoking hot
7. Finally the lyrics were made avaiable
8. Girls Locker Room: P.S. Why is women dancing so hot?
9. Boys Locker Room
10. Fuck your blinds
11
12. Good guys England
13. This cat is probably your spirit animal
14. Cost of burgers is getting ridiculous
15. Thanks to the two people (Hi, Mom and Dad!) who made it to the end of this dump. When I'm not busy making questionable life choices on the internet, I've been pouring my soul into a little passion project. If you got a kick out of this dump, you're going to love my game: "Do Not Press The Button (To Delete The Multiverse)." Think "The Stanley Parable" but with even more chaos and quirky characters such this little nihilist pancake. Can you imagine? A first-person game mixed with humor? Anyway, please wishlist: https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse/ It would mean the world to me and my brother if you checked out the game and gave it a wishlist. It won't disappoint. I promise. Just, whatever you do, don't press the big red button!
item.dc:creator: TheseusGames
